text: Healthcare News | Medical Research | Docwire News Hantavirus Outbreak at Sea: How Worried Should You Be? In this interview, Markalain Dery, DO, LCMC Health, explains the ongoing hantavirus crisis at sea, using a recent cruise ship cluster to highlight an unusual Andes hantavirus event.

Determining Endocrine Refractoriness and When to Transition to ADCs The panelists discuss when endocrine therapy stops working and how to choose ADCs in ER+/HER2– breast cancer. Managing Co‑Mutations and Considering Combination Strategies The expert panel discusses treatment strategies for ESR1‑mutated breast cancer with PI3K, AKT, or PTEN co‑mutations.

Comparing Oral SERDs with Insights from EMERALD and EMBER‑3 Comparing elacestrant vs imlunestrant, discussed with insights from EMERALD, EMBER‑3, and real‑world experience. Rilparencel Associated With Slowdown in Kidney Function Decline A phase 2 trial found rilparencel slowed kidney decline by 78% in diabetes-related CKD with an acceptable safety profile.

Practical Approaches to ESR1 Testing with ctDNA and Tissue The panel shares practical guidance on when and how to test for ESR1 in ER+ breast cancer using ctDNA or tissue biopsy. Expert Exchange: ESR1 Mutations in ER+/HER2- Metastatic Breast Cancer Expert roundtable series on ESR1‑mutated ER+/HER2– metastatic breast cancer, covering testing, oral SERDs, and sequencing.

ESR1 Mutations in ER+/HER2- mBC: Biology, Clinical Relevance, and Patient Framing A panel of experts discuss the biology and clinical importance of ESR1 mutations in ER+/HER2– metastatic breast cancer. Once-Daily Inhaler May Offer Modest Advantage for Patients With COPD Once-daily umeclidinium-vilanterol inhalers may have an edge in reducing COPD flare-ups without added safety concerns.
